title: buxus microphylla var. kitashimae
intro: Buxus microphylla var. kitashimae, commonly known as Japanese boxwood, is a variety of the boxwood shrub native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal activities. In herbal preparations, Buxus microphylla var. kitashimae is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
